ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn entry exists for Pani Puri Pizza specifically. FOODON_00002044 (pizza food product) is used as the closest parent category covering the pizza base component.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Pizza) is used as the closest parent. No specific code exists for this Indian-Italian fusion variant.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for Pani Puri Pizza or a sufficiently close fusion food category.

serving.natural_language_portion: One personal-sized Pani Puri Pizza of approximately 300 g, equivalent to a standard restaurant serving at Sultan Palace Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated from component analysis: Pizza Margherita (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy #21299) and Pani Puri / Golgappa chaat components (ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods, 2017 edition)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (SR Legacy) and ICMR-NIN
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry exists for Pani Puri Pizza in any approved primary source. Nutrients estimated by weighted component blending: approximately 60% pizza base with cheese and tomato sauce (USDA SR Legacy pizza margherita values) and 40% pani puri chaat topping (ICMR-NIN values for golgappa/pani puri with chickpea filling and chutneys). The blend reflects the typical restaurant preparation observed at Sultan Palace Singapore. Confidence is medium due to estimation.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of pizza and refined wheat flatbread products; analogous estimate based on Pizza (GI ~60–68) from Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: Diabetes Care (American Diabetes Association peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Pani Puri Pizza. GI estimated at 65 based on the published GI range for pizza (60–68, medium GI) from the International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care). The pani puri chaat topping (chickpeas, tamarind chutney) would moderately lower the overall GI relative to plain pizza due to legume and acidic components; however, the refined wheat pizza base dominates the glycemic response. GI of 65 is a conservative mid-range estimate. Glycemic load per 300 g serving = (65 × 30.5 × 3) / 100 = 59.5.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~420 mg/100 g; ~1260 mg per serving) from cheese, tomato sauce, chaat masala, and tamarind chutney
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit Pani Puri Pizza to occasional consumption and request reduced-salt preparation, as a single serving may provide more than 50% of the recommended daily sodium intake of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index (~65) driven by refined wheat pizza base; moderate carbohydrate load (~91.5 g per 300 g serving)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume Pani Puri Pizza in small portions and pair it with a non-starchy vegetable side to blunt the postprandial glycemic response, given its medium GI and high glycemic load per serving.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from mozzarella cheese (~3.1 g/100 g) and elevated sodium content contribute to cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should treat Pani Puri Pizza as an occasional indulgence, limiting portion size to reduce saturated fat and sodium intake, and preferring preparations with reduced-fat cheese and minimal added salt.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pani Puri Pizza is a creative Indian-Italian fusion dish that combines a baked pizza base with the flavours and toppings of pani puri, a beloved Indian street food (chaat). The pizza base is made from refined wheat flour, topped with tomato sauce and melted mozzarella cheese in the conventional Italian style, and then finished with signature pani puri elements: crispy semolina puri shells, spiced chickpea and potato filling, tangy tamarind chutney, vibrant mint-coriander chutney, diced onion, and a sprinkle of chaat masala. The result is a dish that is simultaneously cheesy and tangy, with bursts of spice, sweetness, and acidity from the chaat components. The texture contrasts soft pizza dough with the crunch of puri shells. In Singapore, this dish is served at Sultan Palace Turkish and Lebanese Cuisine restaurant at Boat Quay, reflecting the multicultural and experimental food culture of the city-state. Nutritionally, it is a moderate-energy food (~218 kcal/100 g) with a medium glycemic index (~65), meaningful sodium content (~420 mg/100 g), and moderate saturated fat from cheese. It is vegetarian and halal-friendly. Clinically, the high sodium per serving is the primary concern for hypertension management, while the medium GI and high glycemic load per serving require portion control for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The dish is not suitable for gluten-free or vegan diets.

confidence_reason: No direct entry for Pani Puri Pizza exists in any approved primary nutritional database. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component blending of USDA SR Legacy pizza margherita data and ICMR-NIN pani puri/chaat component data. GI is an analogous estimate based on published pizza GI values from a peer-reviewed journal. Confidence is medium rather than low because both component sources are approved primary sources and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: 1. Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reliably reported for either the pizza or pani puri components in the source databases used. 2. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy pizza entry or ICMR-NIN pani puri data used for estimation. 3. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the component sources. 4. All other -1 values reflect nutrients not reported in the approved sources used for this estimation. 5. The cuisine tag in the input JSON lists 'Turkish' (from the restaurant context at Sultan Palace), but the dish itself is an Indian-Italian fusion creation; cuisine has been corrected to 'Fusion (Indian-Italian)' to reflect the food's actual identity. 6. GI of 65 is a conservative analogous estimate; actual GI may vary depending on the specific recipe and proportions used by the restaurant. 7. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_100g × serving_size_g) / 100 = (65 × 30.5 × 300) / 100 = 595.25 / 10 = 59.5 (rounded). 8. Halal status is assumed based on the restaurant context (Sultan Palace, a Turkish and Lebanese halal restaurant in Singapore); kosher status is not confirmed.

Corrected cuisine from 'Turkish' (restaurant context) to 'Fusion (Indian-Italian)' (food identity). Set GI=65 from peer-reviewed analogous estimate. Calculated GL per 300g serving. All hard gates and Q1-Q8 criteria verified as passing.
